items.author: Mike Vorkunov
items.body_excerpt:
NBA had 7 teams taxpayers for 2025-26 season, while 23 teams each got a $4.85 million payout. 

Cavaliers owe NBA-leading $68.671 million in tax payments. Warriors will pay $67.91 million as a repeater tax payer. Knicks owe $44.44 million after finishing $19.5 mil over tax line.

items.title: News: Hinkle Fieldhouse, on the campus of Butler University, will host the 2026 NBA Cup Championship game on Dec. 11.
items.author: Scott Agness
items.body_excerpt:
News: Hinkle Fieldhouse, on the campus of Butler University, will host the 2026 NBA Cup Championship game on Dec. 11.

It had been held at T-Mobile Arena in Las Vegas the past three seasons. Now, it's centrally located. But half as many seats.

items.title: Miami Heat salary cap situation after signing Tim Hardaway Jr.
items.author: Yossi Gozlan
items.body_excerpt:
Miami Heat salary cap situation after signing Tim Hardaway Jr.

His $6.5M salary will go into the mid-level exception.

They have $8.5M remaining from it but can probably use closer to $7 million due to their first apron hard cap proximity after presumably signing Ryan Conwell.

items.title: Kam Jones waived, no picks gained in the transaction (just second round swaps), and the roster spot point isn't viable because the offseason limit is 20 players. They sold a high second-round pick for cash at the start of a rebuild, again. This is just what the Bulls do.
items.author: Ricky O'Donnell
items.body_excerpt: Kam Jones waived, no picks gained in the transaction (just second round swaps), and the roster spot point isn't viable because the offseason limit is 20 players. They sold a high second-round pick for cash at the start of a rebuild, again. This is just what the Bulls do.

items.title: The offer Quentin Grimes' former agent emphatically claimed the Sixers made last year: four years, $39 million. Sixers denied it, claiming there was never a formal offer because the gap was so large. Grimes played on $8.7 million qualifying offer last year, then changed agents.

items.title: The NBA salary cap will go up by $10.314 million for the 2026-27 season — a 6.669% increase from this past season.
items.author: Mike Vorkunov
items.body_excerpt:
The NBA salary cap will go up by $10.314 million for the 2026-27 season — a 6.669% increase from this past season.

First apron: $209.015 million.

Second apron: $221.686 million.

Non-taxpayer MLE: $15.044 million
Taxpayer MLE: $6.064 million
Room exception: $9.366 million

items.title: NBA announces their official salary cap numbers for the 2026-27 season. Slightly lower than the last projections reported in March. Pacers are over the cap, obviously. With 13 players:
items.author: Tony East
items.body_excerpt:
NBA announces their official salary cap numbers for the 2026-27 season. Slightly lower than the last projections reported in March. Pacers are over the cap, obviously. With 13 players:

-$1.826MM under the tax
-$9.933MM under the first apron

items.title: The Chicago Bulls will fully guarantee the $2.41 million salary of Leonard Miller for the 2026-27 season, league sources told HoopsHype. Miller averaged 11.7 points on 55.5% shooting from the field with 5.8 rebounds in 27 games, including 12 starts, for Chicago last season.
items.author: Michael Scotto
items.body_excerpt: The Chicago Bulls will fully guarantee the $2.41 million salary of Leonard Miller for the 2026-27 season, league sources told HoopsHype. Miller averaged 11.7 points on 55.5% shooting from the field with 5.8 rebounds in 27 games, including 12 starts, for Chicago last season.

items.title: Final 2026 cap space projections:
items.author: KeithSmithNBA
items.body_excerpt:
Final 2026 cap space projections:

1. Lakers: $51.2M
2. Nets: $40.9M (if they delay adding J. Randle, $30.9 if they don't)
3. Bulls: $30.9M

Pistons: Over for now, $26.3M w/ Duren cap hold, $44.4M w/o Duren cap hold

Clippers: Over for now, could create up to $31M in cap space

items.title: Final cap number is down a hair from the estimate of $165M, but not enough difference to matter.
items.author: John Hollinger
items.body_excerpt: Final cap number is down a hair from the estimate of $165M, but not enough difference to matter.

items.title: NBA salary cap figures are here. The league's 2026-27 fiscal year has arrived.
items.author: Rod Boone
items.body_excerpt: NBA salary cap figures are here. The league's 2026-27 fiscal year has arrived.

items.title: The Chicago Bulls plan to waive Kam Jones, league sources told HoopsHype. Jones had a deadline today that would’ve fully guaranteed his salary at $2.15 million. He was the 38th pick in the 2025 NBA Draft by the Indiana Pacers and was traded to Chicago on draft night.
items.author: Michael Scotto
items.body_excerpt: The Chicago Bulls plan to waive Kam Jones, league sources told HoopsHype. Jones had a deadline today that would’ve fully guaranteed his salary at $2.15 million. He was the 38th pick in the 2025 NBA Draft by the Indiana Pacers and was traded to Chicago on draft night.

items.title: NBA officially announced that the salary cap is $164.961 million for the 2026-27 season.
items.author: Brad Rowland
items.body_excerpt:
NBA officially announced that the salary cap is $164.961 million for the 2026-27 season.

Tax is $200.428 million.

items.title: The Detroit Pistons have offered what the franchise believes is the most lucrative contract possible for their All-Star restricted free agent Jalen Duren, are not interested in any sign and trade deals and will match any potential offer sheet he signs, sources @espn @andscape.
items.author: MarcJSpears
items.body_excerpt: The Detroit Pistons have offered what the franchise believes is the most lucrative contract possible for their All-Star restricted free agent Jalen Duren, are not interested in any sign and trade deals and will match any potential offer sheet he signs, sources @espn @andscape.

items.title: Catching up on some earlier deals today:
items.author: KeithSmithNBA
items.body_excerpt:
Catching up on some earlier deals today:

🏀Hawks will have to use the NTMLE ($14M of it) to re-sign Jock Landale
🏀Hawks likely used most of the Clint Capela TPE ($6.7M) to bring in Devin Carter ($5.1M)
🏀Hawks are $12.2M under the tax

1/2
